subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The exploration follows a clear logical sequence with explicit transitions between introduction, data collection, analysis and conclusion.
Section headings, a contents page and consistent sub-headings provide a recognisable and navigable structure.
Tables, graphs and formulae are placed adjacent to the relevant text, facilitating reader comprehension.
weaknesses: Inclusion of lengthy background definitions and the full chi-square table adds unnecessary bulk.
Minor digressions (e.g. extensive copied definitions) interrupt the flow and could be trimmed.
Some sections (bullet-lists of empirical percentages) could be condensed into concise tables for relevance.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands scored: coherence and development at band 3, organization at band 3, conciseness at band 2. The report is coherent and well organized but includes unnecessary material that prevents it reaching the highest band. A holistic score of 3/4 reflects these strengths and minor weaknesses.

strengths: Appropriate use of mathematical notation (\bar{x}, Ï, ÏÂ²) with clear explanations accompanying each symbol.
Effective employment of multiple representations: tables, histograms, a superimposed normal curve and shaded pdf.
Logical flow of mathematical argument with step-by-step calculations for the ÏÂ² test.
weaknesses: Occasional notation inconsistencies (mixing â¤ and <, using â0_iâ instead of âO_iâ).
Low-resolution figures with faint axis labels and lack of explicit scale justification.
Verbose formula blocks could be streamlined or moved to an appendix.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three communication strands meet band 3 descriptors: mostly consistent notation, effective multiple representations, and good logical clarity. Occasional slips and presentation issues prevent a band 4. A holistic score of 3/4 accurately reflects these observations.

strengths: Primary data collection and decisions on class merging show clear initiative and independent decision-making.
Personal rationale linking the investigation to curiosity about supermarket produce demonstrates a personal approach.
Suggestions for future extensions reveal thoughtful engagement with methodology.
weaknesses: The approach follows a conventional statistical template with limited novelty or innovative modelling.
Creativity and initiative, while evident, are not sustained at an exceptional level.
Independent thinking is significant but not outstanding, lacking a higher level of originality.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each personal engagement strand aligns with band 2: significant independent thinking, clear personal approach, and good but not exceptional creativity. A holistic score of 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on sampling limitations and reasons for deviation from normality.
Thoughtful analysis comparing the ÏÂ² statistic to the critical value with correct interpretation.
Clear evaluation of outcomes with concrete suggestions for improving sampling and methodology.
weaknesses: Reflection is largely confined to the conclusion rather than integrated throughout the report.
Critical analysis remains at a basic level without exploring alternative models or deeper error analysis.
Graphical observations (skewness, bimodality) are not fully woven into the critical reflection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All reflection strands sit at band 2: meaningful but not deep reflection, basic critical analysis, and clear but limited evaluation of outcomes. A holistic score of 2/3 reflects this consistent mid-band performance.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (mean, standard deviation, empirical rule, pdf construction, ÏÂ² test) effectively addresses the research question.
Mathematics is fully commensurate with SL course level, demonstrating sound application of normal distribution and hypothesis testing.
Computations are largely accurate and correctly interpreted (mean = 237.55 g, Ï = 21.07 g, ÏÂ² = 10.999, df = 5).
weaknesses: Minor transcription and notation errors (missing formula for Ï, unconventional hypothesis statements).
Uneven class-interval merging and axis-limit choices lack full mathematical justification.
Opportunity to deepen analysis (e.g. quantitative misfit measures, impact on degrees of freedom) was not taken.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics strands each align with band 4: relevant mathematics used effectively, fully course-appropriate level, and mostly correct understanding. Minor errors and missed opportunities for deeper mathematical discussion limit performance to 4/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany every calculation, and step-by-step working is shown for the ÏÂ² test.  The narrative is generally clear, though some formula blocks (e.g., the empirical-rule percentages) are verbose.  Overall the logical structure of the mathematical argument is good and understandable.

Computations (mean 237.55 g, Ï 21.07 g, expected frequencies, ÏÂ² = 10.999, df = 5) are largely accurate and correctly interpreted.  Minor transcription slips and symbol errors indicate that accuracy is âmostly correctâ rather than flawless, so band 4 is appropriate.

The student employs tables, histograms, a superimposed normal curve and a shaded pdf, covering several representation types effectively.  While all graphs are relevant, scale choices are only briefly justified and some figures are low-resolution, so the use is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effective.â

The exploration follows a clear sequence (introduction â data collection â graphical analysis â hypothesis test â conclusion).  Transitions between sections are explicit and each section logically builds on results from the previous one.  Minor digressions (e.g., lengthy definitions copied from websites) occasionally interrupt the flow, preventing the report from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Gathering primary data, merging classes appropriately and superimposing curves show initiative, yet creativity is limited to standard statistical tools.  There is no particularly novel modelling or sophisticated innovation to justify the highest band.

Results are interpreted correctly (e.g., comparison of ÏÂ² statistic with critical value) and basic implications are discussed.  Analysis is sound but not deeply critical; alternative models or error analysis are not pursued, so the strand fits the second band.

The student evaluates sampling method and sample size and proposes concrete improvements and extensions.  These points demonstrate a clear evaluation of outcomes and implications, aligning with band 2 rather than the comprehensive depth expected for band 3.

The student reflects on why the data might deviate from normality and recognises sampling limitations.  Reflection appears mainly in the conclusion, but comments are thoughtful and linked to results, satisfying the descriptor for âmeaningful reflection.â

Notation for mean (\bar{x}), standard deviation (Ï), class bounds and ÏÂ² is correctly used and explained.  Occasional slips (mixing \le and <, writing 0_i instead of O_i) prevent complete consistency, but overall the mathematical language is appropriate and mostly accurate.

The rationale links the investigation to the studentâs curiosity about supermarket produce and includes suggestions for future extensions.  These elements demonstrate a clear personal approach, but the analysis itself remains conventional, so the strand fits the middle band.

Most material is relevant to answering the research question, but explanations of basic concepts (normal curves, Î±âlevels, etc.) and large copied tables add unnecessary length.  Hence the report is "mostly relevant with some unnecessary elements" rather than fully concise.

All mathematics directly addresses the research question: calculation of mean/Ï, use of empirical rule, construction of pdf, and a correctly executed ÏÂ² GoF test.  The range is appropriate and contributes effectively to conclusions, matching the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ descriptor.

Choosing a self-collected data set and deciding to verify normality with both graphical and ÏÂ² methods evidences personal decision-making.  Nevertheless, the approach follows a standard textbook template and shows only âsignificantâ (not outstanding) independent thinking.

A contents page, section headings and consistent sub-headings give the piece a solid, recognisable structure.  Tables, graphs and formulae are placed close to the text that references them, so the reader can follow the investigation with little difficulty.  A few pages (e.g., the full chi-square table) add bulk without aiding navigation, so the organisation is good rather than exceptional.

Normal distribution analysis and ÏÂ² testing are fully within AA SL expectations and are applied in a way that clearly demonstrates understanding.  The mathematics is therefore âfully commensurate with course level,â but not extended enough for the top two bands.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is overly detailed, including minor subsections that clutter navigation. Consider consolidating entries and focusing on main sections to improve conciseness and reader orientation.
The introduction contains lengthy background copied from sources without synthesis. Trim redundant definitions and focus on your rationale to enhance coherence and maintain reader engagement.
The sampling description is clear but lacks critical reflection at this stage. Introduce brief commentary on potential bias from using a single crate to demonstrate deeper awareness of methodology.

In the frequency table class intervals mix â<â and ââ¤â inconsistently, and notation for observed frequency appears as â0ââ in formulae. Ensure consistent use of $$<$$ vs $$\le$$ and correct symbol notation throughout.
The histogram image is low resolution and axes labels are faint. Improve figure quality and explicitly justify scale choices to enhance clarity and support your analysis.
The text describing skewness uses informal language and does not link observation to later statistical tests. Tighten mathematical language and connect visual skewness to hypothesis outcomes.

The standard deviation value is quoted as produced by the GDC without showing the formula. Display the calculation or formula $$\sigma=\sqrt{\frac{\sum(x-\bar x)^2}{n}}$$ to strengthen mathematical rigor.
The empirical rule bullets are described in prose only. Reflect on reasons for the observed 75% vs theoretical 68% and 91.67% vs 95%, linking deviation to sampling or distributional assumptions.
Listing empirical percentages in bullet form increases length. Consider summarizing the 68â95â99.7% checks in a concise table to aid readability and relevance.
Graph 2âs caption omits parameter values. Add mean and standard deviation on the figure or in caption to reinforce mathematical communication of the normal curve.
The narrative separates graphical observations from the final test. Integrate comments on skewness and bimodality into your evaluation to deepen critical reflection.
The description of the GDC function $$\mathrm{normPdf}(x,\bar x,\sigma)$$ lacks specification of domain and units. Explicitly define input ranges and units to improve clarity.
Adjusting min/max x-values without justification may mislead. Provide reasoning or calculate support points (e.g., meanÂ±3Ï) to justify axis limits mathematically.
The superimposed histogram and curve are interpreted qualitatively. Quantify misfit (e.g., area difference or KS statistic) to strengthen the connection between visual and statistical assessments.
The paragraph ends abruptly and seems incomplete, disrupting flow. Ensure the text is complete and provides a full conclusion or transition to the hypothesis test.
The null and alternative hypotheses are conventionally reversed: typically $$H_0$$ assumes normality. Align hypothesis statements with standard conventions to avoid confusion.
The criteria table lists âNot yet metâ for expected frequencies before merging. Update the table after combining intervals to reflect accurate met status.
Merging class intervals yields uneven widths and reduces df; discuss the impact on test sensitivity and degrees of freedom to show comprehensive use of mathematics.
The large table of expected frequencies and probabilities could be streamlined by focusing on final merged intervals only. Move detailed interval calculations to appendix.
In the chi-square calculation block, the notation uses â0_iâ instead of â$$O_i$$â. Correct this typographical error to maintain clear mathematical communication.
The degrees of freedom definition is uncited in text. Include an in-text citation for the formula $$df=k-1$$ to strengthen academic rigor in mathematical communication.
The extensive chi-square distribution table in the body disrupts flow. Relocate the full table to an appendix or summary section, citing only relevant critical values in the main text.
The conclusion succinctly evaluates sampling bias and test outcomes, demonstrating meaningful reflection on limitations and improvements.
The recommendations for increasing sample size and expanding crates show personal engagement and initiative in refining methodology.
The raw data table is appropriately placed in the appendix, keeping the main report concise and focused on analysis.
The distribution-vs-weights table lacks clear axis labels or units, making interpretation difficult. Add headings and units for each column to improve presentation.
Figure 1âs legend and units are missing. Include axis labels, units (g for weight), and a legend explaining curve vs data points for full mathematical communication.
